title: Docker Buildx CI, Tests, Context, Cache, and Multi-Platform Output · Release Engineering Workshop
summary: A delivery pairing session predicts each automation transition before producing the first working container build and test workflow for docker buildx ci, tests, context, cache, and multi-platform output. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of container build and test workflow while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Configure official Docker setup and build actions with explicit Git or path context, Dockerfile, target, platforms, inputs, and output mode.
Use secret mounts, scoped cache backends, deterministic keys, trusted writers, and no credential-bearing ARG, layer, log, or attestation.
Test image configuration, non-root and read-only behavior, health, signals, resources, network, changed config, and failure before registry mutation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Ci Buildx Action Context Contract
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Buildx workflow leaks a secret through ARG, trusts a fork cache, builds only amd64, pushes before tests, and cannot tie output to source. Separate builder, context, platforms, cache, test, secret, and digest evidence.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Ci Buildx Action Context Contract.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Ci Build Record Summary Debug
steps.instruction:
Append the CI/CD evidence item headed "  # Evidence: ci-build-record-summary-debug-lbopku". Tailor its container-build-workflow.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic workflow or release review, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run GitHub Actions, runner, Docker, registry, OIDC, Google Cloud, and deployment work later in authorized disposable transfer environments.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Ci Build Record Summary Debug.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Ci Build Record Summary Debug into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Ci Build Record Summary Debug, keep Ci Build Record Summary Debug connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-build-record-summary-debug-lbopku
  - competency: ci-build-record-summary-debug
    artifact: container-build-workflow.yaml
    decision: build record summary debug
    environment: multi-platform Buildx release to a non-production registry
    fault-injection: remove one supported Node and operating-system matrix cell
    retained-proof: queue duration critical path failure class recovery and cost evidence
    case-key: lbopku
    change: |
      setup-buildx-action: 4
      build-push-action: 7
      context: explicit-path-after-generation
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
      platforms: [linux-amd64, linux-arm64]
      secret: buildkit-secret-mount
      cache-writer: trusted-main-only
      push: after-platform-tests
      build-record: retained
      output-digest: sha256-verified
    verify: render build inputs inspect build record test each platform changed config signals health and read-only behavior then verify index digest
    failure: Reject implicit Git context, ARG secrets, fork-writable cache, build-only proof, one-platform tests, or push-before-validation.
    repair: Select context deliberately, mount secrets, scope cache, test runtime behavior per platform, retain records, and push verified output.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Workshop Ci Build Record Summary Debug

content.title: Evidence defense
